grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements row 232; paper failure mode domestic_violence
grounding_note: Expansion batch: more abuse-vs-schedule and agency-involvement disclosures. Safety-aware wording.

scenarios.opening_query: I need to change our parenting plan. Exchanges with my ex have become impossible.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Restraining Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: At the last exchange he shoved me against the car with our son watching and said next time I'd 'go through the windshield.' I want protection, not just a new schedule.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Last exchange he shoved me against the car in front of our son and threatened worse. I want protection, not just a schedule.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether exchange conflict involves violence or threats
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No violence — he moved an hour away and the midpoint exchange plan just doesn't work anymore.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No violence — he moved an hour away and the exchange plan just doesn't work now.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Impossible exchanges' is the exact euphemism DV screening should probe.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter comes back from her dad's house withdrawn and with stories that worry me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: Her school counselor made a mandatory report last week — DHS has opened an assessment on his household.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Her school counselor made a mandatory report — DHS opened an assessment on his household.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a child-welfare assessment is open
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No agency involved — the 'stories' are about his new girlfriend's harsh rules, and I want the plan revisited.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No agency — it's his girlfriend's harsh rules, and I want the plan revisited.
scenarios.vetting_note: Mandatory-report fact moves the case into the dependency system.

scenarios.opening_query: I want a protective order for my kids against their mother's new boyfriend.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's never harmed or threatened them — I just don't like his record from years ago; the honest ask is custody conditions about who's around the kids.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's never harmed or threatened them — it's his old record that scares me. Really I want custody conditions about who's around the kids.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether qualifying conduct exists or the concern belongs in custody conditions
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Last month he grabbed my son hard enough to bruise and the pediatrician documented it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He grabbed my son hard enough to bruise last month — the pediatrician documented it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Tests the over-ask direction: fear without qualifying conduct is a custody-conditions case.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex quit paying child support the day he got remarried and now claims he 'can't afford' our kids.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Support/Modification || Debtor/Creditor > Judgment Collection
scenarios.hidden_fact: The state already reduced the arrears to a money judgment of $31,000 — what I need now is collection: garnishment, liens, whatever reaches his new business.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The arrears are already a $31,000 money judgment — I need collection now: garnishment, liens, whatever reaches his business.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether arrears are already reduced to judgment needing enforcement
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No judgment yet — he filed to modify support downward and I have three weeks to respond.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No judgment — he filed to reduce the support and I have three weeks to respond.
scenarios.vetting_note: Judgment-in-hand converts a family-court matter into enforcement practice.

scenarios.opening_query: The other parent keeps our child from me every time we disagree about anything.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Paternity
scenarios.hidden_fact: We were never married and paternity was never legally established — I learned I have nothing to enforce until that's done.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We never married and paternity was never established — so there's nothing to enforce until that's done.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We divorced two years ago with a full parenting plan — she's violating a real court order.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We're divorced with a full parenting plan — she's violating a real order.
scenarios.vetting_note: Parentage-gap fact again from the father's enforcement side; distinct from 16-14's support angle.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm scared for my sister and her kids. Her husband controls every part of their lives.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.)
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's told me clearly: there's been no violence or threats, and what she wants is a divorce lawyer and a plan to leave safely with the kids and some money.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's clear there's been no violence or threats — she wants a divorce lawyer and a safe exit plan with the kids and some money.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what relief the affected person actually seeks
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's threatened to hurt her if she leaves and monitors her phone — she's asked me to help her get a protective order first.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's threatened to hurt her if she leaves and tracks her phone — she wants the protective order first.
scenarios.vetting_note: Third-party helper voice; the affected person's own stated goal routes it. Coercive control without threats is deliberately kept on the divorce side per FAPA's conduct requirements.

scenarios.opening_query: My teenage son refuses to go to his mother's and the court order says he has to.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: He finally told his therapist why — her boyfriend hits him — and the therapist's mandatory report has DHS interviewing him tomorrow.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He told his therapist her boyfriend hits him — the mandatory report means DHS interviews him tomorrow.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether abuse disclosure and agency involvement changed the posture
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing like that — he's sixteen with a job and a girlfriend here, and the every-weekend schedule doesn't fit his life; we need a modification.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing like that — he's sixteen with a job and girlfriend here; the schedule just doesn't fit his life anymore.
scenarios.vetting_note: Teen-refusal cases split exactly this way in practice; the disclosure is the fork.

scenarios.opening_query: Grandma and I have been raising my nephew since spring and school registration is impossible without papers.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Guardianship || Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: He came to us through a DHS safety plan when his mom was arrested — the dependency case is active and we're only 'temporary placement' in their system.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He came through a DHS safety plan when his mom was arrested — the dependency case is active and we're just 'temporary placement.'
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the child is in an active dependency case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No agency involved — his mom dropped him off and signed nothing; we need guardianship so we can enroll him and get him to a doctor.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No agency — his mom just left him with us and signed nothing. We need guardianship for school and doctors.
scenarios.vetting_note: Kinship-care routing depends entirely on whether DHS holds the case.
